open_known_gaps.summary: Client-side memory-exhaustion (PDF-bomb DoS) class — still tracked, no server impact. Two load boundaries share this gap: (1) viewer.js loadDocument() (getDocument().promise → PDF_LOADED) has no numPages>1000 cap and no processing timeout, so a crafted high-page-count PDF within the 50MB limit renders one canvas+thumbnail per page (linear footprint; the render-race amplifier was FIXED+VERIFIED in TASK-316). (2) NEW this tick: merge.js (TASK-319) validates each queued file independently and enforces the per-file 50MB limit, but has NO cap on cumulative queued bytes or combined page count — a user can queue many ~50MB files and mergeAndDownload() loads them all into pdf-lib at once, exceeding the 200MB total-memory guideline. Both client-side only (a user can only exhaust their own tab). Fix family = developer task: add a page-count/byte cap at each load boundary. Documented in docs/security-guide.md.
